channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ADC Criticises Tinubu’s Economic Policies Amid Rising Poverty to 63%
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) don yan say President Bola Tinubu economic policies never dey work well as poverty don rise sharply to 63% since dem comot fuel subsidy. ADC National Publicity Se...

item.title: Tanzania Seeks More Angel Investors to Support Early-Stage Startups
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania’s startup scene dey grow strong, especially for fintech, agri-tech, logistics and digital services, but young entrepreneurs still dey struggle with lack of early-stage capital...

item.title: Teenage driver sentenced to 14 months' youth detention for causing deaths of two passengers in high-speed crash
item.description: An 18-year-old driver, Madeleine Lonsdale, has been sentenced to 14 months in youth detention after pleading guilty to two counts of causing death by careless or inconsiderate driving. The incident oc...

item.title: Alex Johnston sets new NRL try-scoring record at Allianz Stadium
item.description: South Sydney's seasoned winger, Alex Johnston, has etched his name into NRL history by scoring his 213th career try, surpassing Ken Irvine’s long-standing record of 212 tries set in 1973. The record-b...

item.title: Pete Hegseth criticises media coverage as US military strikes Iran amid Strait of Hormuz tensions
item.description: On 13 March 2026, Pete Hegseth criticised the media for what he described as 'fake headlines' regarding the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran, particularly concerning incidents in th...

item.title: Having Both Adenomas and Serrated Polyps Significantly Increases Risk of Advanced Colorectal Neoplasia
item.description: Researchers from Flinders University and Flinders Medical Centre conducted a large-scale review of over 8,400 colonoscopy records to investigate the cancer risks associated with common bowel polyps. T...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Mineral Trading with 120 Official Buying Centres
item.description: Tanzania has seen big improvements in its mining sector after reforms that followed the 2017 Mining Act amendments. According to the Minerals Ministry, the country now has 44 mineral markets and 120 b...

item.title: Court Temporarily Reinstates President Ruto’s Advisory Team Amid Legal Challenge
item.description: President William Ruto has gained temporary relief after the Court of Appeal in Nairobi reinstated his 21-member advisory team. The appellate bench, composed of Justices William Korir, Hedwig Ong’udi,...

item.title: Alex Eala Reaches No. 31 in WTA Rankings, Marking a Milestone in Philippine Tennis
item.description: Filipino tennis star Alex Eala has achieved a significant milestone by climbing to the 31st spot in the Women’s Tennis Association (WTA) world rankings. This marks a momentous achievement not just for...

item.title: A history of unforgettable Oscar red carpet outfits through the decades
item.description: The article from The Guardian revisits some of the most memorable and unconventional outfits ever worn on the Oscars red carpet. It begins with Björk’s 2001 swan dress, designed by Marjan Pejoski, a l...

item.title: Genetic Insights Offer Hope for Accurate Diagnosis and Treatment of Progressive Supranuclear Palsy
item.description: The late Rev. Jesse Jackson, who passed away at 84 in February 2026, experienced a misdiagnosis that highlights the difficulty of distinguishing progressive supranuclear palsy (PSP) from Parkinson’s d...

item.title: Andhra Pradesh hoteliers face LPG shortage; advised to adjust operations
item.description: Amid the ongoing scarcity of cooking gas in Andhra Pradesh, Finance Minister Payyavula Keshav has recommended that hotels consider rotational or alternate day operations to manage the shortage effecti...

item.title: Lachie Lonergan returns to action as Corey Toole ruled out of Brumbies vs Drua match
item.description: The ACT Brumbies are managing a mix of comebacks and injury setbacks ahead of their upcoming Super Rugby Pacific clash with the Fijian Drua. Lachie Lonergan is set to play for the Brumby Runners on Sa...

item.title: Emma Heming Willis establishes dementia research fund to support families and caregivers
item.description: Emma Heming Willis has launched the Emma & Bruce Willis Fund for Dementia Research and Caregiver Support in honour of her husband, Bruce Willis, who was diagnosed with frontotemporal dementia (FTD) in...

item.title: Newly released photograph shows Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Lord Mandelson with Jeffrey Epstein in Martha’s Vineyard
item.description: A new image has emerged showing Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Lord Peter Mandelson seated with the late financier and convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein, both wearing white bathrobes. The photo, re...

item.title: The Princess of Wales engages with Londoners during visits to Borough Market and RNLI Lifeboat Station
item.description: The Princess of Wales, Kate Middleton, spent a busy day in London alongside Prince William, engaging in a series of unannounced public appearances. Their day included visits to Bermondsey Beer Mile, w...

item.title: Canada to Continue Russian Oil Sanctions Despite Temporary U.S. Waiver
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney confirmed that Canada will continue enforcing its sanctions on Russian oil exports, including measures targeting the shadow fleet of oil transporters, even as the U...

item.title: Woman found dead in Murang’a home with blood drained into bucket, police investigate
item.description: A woman has been found dead in her house in Murang’a County under very suspicious circumstances after a villager reported that her blood had been drained into a bucket. The incident happened in Gatung...

item.title: Coltide Luhwago’s Journey to Leadership in Claims and Compliance
item.description: Coltide Luhwago, Claims Manager at Tanzania East Africa Gateway Terminal Limited (TEAGTL), is known for her dedication, integrity, and fairness in the field of insurance and risk management. Starting ...

item.title: How Russian Mercenaries Are Making West Africa More Insecure
item.description: For West Africa, di security palava no dey easy again. From Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger reach Nigeria, di region dey face wahala wey no be small because of jihadi groups like Boko Haram, ISWAP, and JNIM...
